raw_response:
{
  "communicative_function": "asserting a normative-metaphysical principle that equates naturalness with non-evil, thereby excluding natural phenomena from moral condemnation",
  "implicit_claims": [
    "a meaningful distinction exists between 'according to nature' and 'contrary to nature'",
    "evil is not an intrinsic or possible property of anything that conforms to nature",
    "nature possesses or participates in an order that is incompatible with evil",
    "moral evaluation can be grounded in conformity to nature"
  ],
  "coherence_assessment": "internally coherent only within a teleological metaphysics that defines nature as ordered toward the good; otherwise risks circularity or equivocation on 'nature'",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": "acceptance of a natural-law or teleological framework in which nature is reliably aligned with goodness or flourishing",
  "conditions_for_misleading": "equivocal use of 'nature' to include destructive or harmful regularities, or application to justify acts whose moral status does not track natural occurrence"
}

parsed_json.communicative_function: asserting a normative-metaphysical principle that equates naturalness with non-evil, thereby excluding natural phenomena from moral condemnation
implicit_claims: a meaningful distinction exists between 'according to nature' and 'contrary to nature'
evil is not an intrinsic or possible property of anything that conforms to nature
nature possesses or participates in an order that is incompatible with evil
moral evaluation can be grounded in conformity to nature

parsed_json.conditions_for_misleading: equivocal use of 'nature' to include destructive or harmful regularities, or application to justify acts whose moral status does not track natural occurrence
